evidence.title: README (segment 1)
README (segment 2)
README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: A high-performance application for analyzing drone flight logs (DJI and Litchi CSV formats). Available as a Tauri v2 desktop app or a Docker-deployable web app. Built with DuckDB and React.

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: - **High-Performance Analytics**: DuckDB-powered queries with automatic downsampling for large datasets. Free, open source, no subscription required. - **Multi-Format Support**: Import DJI logs (.txt), Litchi CSV, and Airdata CSV exports with automatic unit detection. Third-party apps (Dronelink, DroneDeploy) supported. - **Smart Deduplication**: Prevents duplicate imports based on drone serial, battery serial, and start time. - **Interactive Flight Maps**: 3D terrain, map-type selection (Satellite, Topographic, OpenStreetMap), flight replay with speed control (0.5x-16x), live telemetry overlay, and RC joystick visualization. - **Telemetry Charts**: Height, speed, battery, cell voltages, attitude, RC signal, GPS, distance-to-home, velocity, battery full capacity, and battery remained capacity with synchronized drag-to-zoom. - **Local-First Storage**: All data in a local DuckDB database. No cloud upload required (except DJI key fetch during first import). - **Smart Tags**: Auto-tagging (Night Flight, High Speed, Low Battery, etc.) and offline reverse geocoding for location tags. Manual tags and bulk operations supported.on. - **Filters & Search**: Date range, drone/battery/controll…

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: You first need to collect the DJI flight log files that you can import to this application. This project supports modern DJI log files in the `.txt` format. For DJI fly apps on Android or RC remotes, they are usually in `Internal Storage > Android > data > dji.go.v5 > files > FlightRecord`. For iOS, Connect your iPhone/iPad to a computer, open iTunes/Finder, select the device, go to the "File Sharing" tab, select the DJI app, and copy the "Logs" folder. If you are already using other online sync applications, you can download the original logs files directly from there too.

> [!IMPORTANT] > ### For Mac users only > If you are using MacOS, and get a `Can't open file` error, please [follow the guide here](https://github.com/arpanghosh8453/dji-logbook/tree/main#setup-and-installation-windowsmacos) to run the application. ## What's New ### Bug Fix : Date format settings improved The date format no longer switches to a language locale. The format and language are now independent from each other and applies throughout the interface. ### Mark decommissioned drones Use a `[X]` in the custom name/label for the drone and they will automatically get grayed out in the interface and hidden from the maintenance section. ### Preserve record customization This version onward, the flight record customizations done by the user (adding notes, color, name, tags) will be preserved when the same log files (with identical file hash) are re-imported after deletion. These entries are kept in a separate table along side the flight metadata columns. ### Supporter badge verification The supporter badge verification logic has been moved to the back-end, so it persists across different browsers (for docker web deployments) and sessions, independent of the localstorage variable va…

> [!IMPORTANT] > ### For Mac users only > If you are using MacOS, and get a `Can't open file` error, please [follow the guide here](https://github.com/arpanghosh8453/dji-logbook/tree/main#setup-and-installation-windowsmacos) to run the application. ## What's New ### Battery capacity mAh chart Flight data structures now include battery full capacity, remained capacity fields. These values are extracted during log parsing and stored for use in telemetry charts and reports. Using this feature requires deleting the old logs from your setup and re-import/sync. ### Controller serial number extraction Added extraction of controller (remote) serial numbers from DJI flight logs, alongside battery capacity value extraction from parsed telemetry data. Using this feature requires deleting the old logs from your setup and re-import/sync.

> [!IMPORTANT] > ### For Mac users only > If you are using MacOS, and get a `Can't open file` error, please [follow the guide here](https://github.com/arpanghosh8453/dji-logbook/tree/main#setup-and-installation-windowsmacos) to run the application. ## What's new ### Added Radial chart for hour of flight A new radian chart view was added based on the flight start time of all your flights in the overview window. This takes the time of your flight in the timezone of the start location - this is helpful to see which time windows you cover the most, and which ones are you missing :) ### Optimized Flight map path rendering for smoother experience Grouped the segments and down-sampled data points for improving responsiveness. ### Persist session token restore via localstorage For web deployments, previously closing tag tab cleared the session token, requiring re-login. Now we store a fall back copy in local storage for session restore - the token is always validated server side and requests re-login after TTL expiry. ## Support this project Maintaining and im…
